fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: MongoDB shows strong revenue growth and healthy free cash flow, but operates with negative profit margins and a high forward P/E ratio.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 12.1%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY indicates strong market demand.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $463.32M demonstrates solid cash generation.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target of $358.82.
negatives: Profit margin is negative at -2.9%, indicating unprofitability.
Forward P/E of 43.38 suggests a high valuation relative to future earnings.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.13 indicates significant leverage.
Return on equity and assets are negative, showing poor capital efficiency.

news.paragraph: MongoDB is set to report its first-quarter earnings on May 28th, with analysts anticipating an 18.4% growth rate for the current quarter. Recent news highlighted Google Cloud's use of Instruqt, a MongoDB platform, for training on Agentic AI at Google Next 2026, suggesting strong enterprise adoption. Despite a significant one-year rally and ongoing analyst optimism, the company's valuation remains a key point of discussion, with a forward P/E of 43.38.
